content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Mr. Robert L. Shand 
city Clerk-Treasurer 
City of Downey 
P.O. Box 607 
Downey, CA 
90241 
Dear Mr. Shand: 
Administration 
322-5660 
Executive/Legal 
322-5901 
May 20, 1980 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Conflict of Interest 
322-6444 
By letter of April 25, 1980, you have inquired whether any 
reporting obligation has been incurred by Joel H. Lubin by reason 
of Mr. Lubin's "Paid Political Announcement" appearing in the 
April 6, 1980, Southeast Daily News. 
Because, as we understand the facts, Mr. Lubin was not a 
candidate, or an official, controlled or primary purpose committee, 
the standard for determining whether he has any reporting obligation 
is whether his communication "expressly advocates" an election 
result. 
We do not believe that the material meets that standard 
as interpreted by the relevant case law. 
Therefore, Mr. Lubin 
has no reporting obligation pursuant to the Political Reform Act. 
If I can be of further assistance to you, please do not 
hesitate to contact me. 
TMS:nrw 
Sincerely, 
7 
)1~~ <: L-Y 
Thomas M. Sobel 
Chief 
Legal Division 


April 25, 1980 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 95804 
Attention: 
Regina Sici1iano-Kutchins 
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator 
Subject: 
Paid Political Announcement 
Gentlemen: 
Enclosed with this letter is a clipping from the daily Southeast 
News under date of Sunday, April 6, 1980. 
The City of Downey conducted a general municipal election on 
April 8, 1980, wherein various named candidates who have been re-
ferred to in the enclosed newspaper clipping ran for election. 
The author of the newspaper article, Joel H. Lubin, was not a 
candidate in the April 8th election. 
To date, no evidence or word of any existing election campaign 
committee involving Mr. Lubin or any possible associates has been 
brought to my attention. 
My question is whether or not the enclosed paid political announce-
ment constitutes an election campaign status, and if so, should it 
involve filing the necessary campaign identification number 
application? Although the newspaper publisher did not indicate 
the cost of the enclosed publication, it has been estimated that 
the expense exceeded $500. 
I am aware that the publication 
appeared on two separate days. 
Very truly yours, 
Robert L. Shand 
City Clerk-Treasurer 
Encl. 
8425 SECOND STREET 
P.O. BOX 607 
DOWNEY, CALIFORNIA 90241 
(213) 861-0361 




PAID POLITICAL ANNOUNCEMENT

sections.question: Whether the enclosed paid political announcement by Joel H. Lubin appearing in the April 6, 1980, Southeast Daily News constitutes an election campaign status requiring filing of a campaign identification number application, given that Mr. Lubin was not a candidate in the April 8, 1980 municipal election and the expense exceeded $500.
sections.conclusion: We do not believe that the material meets that standard as interpreted by the relevant case law. Therefore, Mr. Lubin has no reporting obligation pursuant to the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a paid political announcement by a non-candidate, non-official published during an election period with expenses exceeding $500 trigger reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that because Mr. Lubin's communication does not "expressly advocate" an election result, no reporting obligation is triggered under the Political Reform Act despite the paid advertisement expense.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear advice letter with both incoming request and FPPC response. Document includes both the April 25, 1980 inquiry from City Clerk Robert L. Shand and the May 20, 1980 FPPC response from Thomas M. Sobel. The actual paid political announcement text is referenced but not fully provided in the document.

QUESTION: Whether the enclosed paid political announcement by Joel H. Lubin appearing in the April 6, 1980, Southeast Daily News constitutes an election campaign status requiring filing of a campaign identification number application, given that Mr. Lubin was not a candidate in the April 8, 1980 municipal election and the expense exceeded $500.

CONCLUSION: We do not believe that the material meets that standard as interpreted by the relevant case law. Therefore, Mr. Lubin has no reporting obligation pursuant to the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that a paid political announcement by non-candidate Joel H. Lubin does not trigger reporting obligations because it does not meet the "express advocacy" standard required under the Political Reform Act, even though the expense exceeded $500.
